description: Display/heading text in various places on the site is too large at mobile widths, causing some text to clip or wrap awkwardly. Scope: (1) audit display-text uses across the site at 375px viewport (home hero, section headings, page titles, etc.); (2) reduce the mobile-side of the responsive type scale for the display tier so it fits without truncation; (3) preserve the desktop sizing — only the mobile breakpoint changes. Check both the Tailwind typography config (or globals.css) and any inline text-display-* utility classes. Acceptance: no display text clips, overflows the viewport, or wraps in a way that breaks the design at 375px width. Mobile-first non-negotiable: this is the primary user experience.
title: Display text too large on mobile — getting cut off
